title: Properties of transverse and longitudinal waves
description: Practice identifying the characteristics of a transverse or longitudinal wave such as the wavelength, crest, and areas of compression.
topic_path: Properties of transverse and longitudinal waves

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is an image of a transverse wave through a medium where the dashed line is the medium's equilibrium position.
[[☃ image 1]]
**What does the circled region represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The circled region shows the part of the wave which is at the maximum distance from the equilibrium in the negative direction.
The correct answer is trough.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is an image of a transverse wave through a medium where the dashed line is the medium's equilibrium position.
[[☃ image 1]]
**What does the circled region represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The circled region shows the part of the wave which is at the maximum distance from the equilibrium in the positive direction.
The correct answer is crest.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is an image of a transverse wave through a medium where the dashed line is the medium's equilibrium position.
[[☃ image 1]]
**What does the arrow represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The arrow is the distance between analogous parts of the wave.  

This shows one full wavelength.
The correct answer is wavelength.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is an image of a transverse wave through a medium where the dashed line is the medium's equilibrium position.
[[☃ image 1]]
**What does the arrow represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The arrow is the distance between analogous parts of the wave.  

This shows one full wavelength.
The correct answer is wavelength.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is an image of a transverse wave through a medium where the dashed line is the medium's equilibrium position.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What does the arrow represent?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints: The arrow shows the maximum displacement of a particle from the mean position.
The correct answer is amplitude.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is an image of a transverse wave through a medium where the dashed line is the medium's equilibrium position.

**Which arrow correctly represents the wavelength?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Wavelength is the distance between analogous parts of the wave. Particles at two ends of the arrow must be at the same displacement from the mean position and moving in the same direction.

Only this arrow has end points that represent analogous points on the wave, as shown below. Particles at the end points have the same displacement from the mean position (positive in this case). The direction of displacement depends on the direction the wave is travelling in.

If the wave is travels to the right, particles at the end points move upwards:

[[☃ image 1]]

If the wave is travels to the left, particles at the end points move downwards:

[[☃ image 2]]

The correct answer is:

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a representation of a longitudinal wave traveling in a uniform medium at a particular instant. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**What does the circled region represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The circled region shows an area where there the lines are relatively far apart, so the medium density is low. Therefore, it is a region of expansion.
The correct answer is expansion.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a representation of a longitudinal wave traveling vertically in a uniform medium at a particular instant. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**What does the circled region represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The circled region shows an area where there the lines are relatively far apart, so the medium density is low. Therefore, it is a region of expansion.
The correct answer is expansion.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a representation of a longitudinal wave traveling in a uniform medium at a particular instant. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**What does the circled region represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The circled region shows an area where the lines are relatively close together, so the medium density is high. Therefore, it is a region of compression.
The correct answer is compression.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a representation of a longitudinal wave traveling in a uniform medium at a particular instant. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**What does the green arrow represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The green arrow is the distance between analogous parts of the wave.  

This shows one full wavelength.
The correct answer is wavelength.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a representation of a longitudinal wave traveling in a uniform medium at a particular instant. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**What does the green arrow represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The green arrow is the distance between analogous parts of the wave.  

This shows one full wavelength.
The correct answer is wavelength.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a representation of a longitudinal wave traveling in a uniform medium at a particular instant. 

**Which arrow correctly represents the wavelength?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
For longitudinal waves, a wavelength is the distance between two consecutive points of maximum compression. 

The wavelength will also equal the distance between two consecutive points of maximum expansion.
The distance between two points will only equal the wavelength if those two points are consecutive and of the exact same phase.

The correct answer is:

[[☃ image 1]]
